description_long: This object is a compact, antique-style shotgun with a short, wide barrel made of metallic material, likely steel, featuring a slightly weathered finish. The handle is ergonomically curved and covered in a reddish-brown wood or wood-like material, adorned with ornate gold detailing at the base and along the grip. The trigger mechanism is exposed, with a visible hammer and a simple, robust trigger guard, suggesting a flintlock or percussion cap firing system. Two gold-colored bands reinforce the barrel, adding both structural integrity and decorative flair. The overall design is reminiscent of historical firearms, with a blend of utilitarian and decorative elements. The object appears solid and heavy, with no visible moving parts except for the hammer and trigger assembly.
description: A compact, antique-style shotgun with a short metallic barrel, ornate gold accents, and a curved reddish-brown handle. The exposed hammer and trigger mechanism evoke a historical firearm aesthetic.
description_view_0: The front view shows the wide, circular barrel opening and the top-mounted sight, with gold accents visible around the barrel.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the full length of the barrel, the ornate handle, and the exposed hammer and trigger mechanism.
description_view_2: The rear view focuses on the gold-embellished handle and the alignment of the barrel, with the trigger guard and hammer partially visible.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the barrel, handle, and mechanical components, with gold detailing on the grip.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, short and wide with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with a simple, unadorned design
